title: K12601: The Configuration utility does not allow the use of exclamation points within an SNMPv3 access password

metadata.concepts: access password ; exclamation ; configuration ; security level ; command ; Reference Guide ; Management Shell ; systems module ; no-auth-no-privacy ; error message
metadata.f5_title: The Configuration utility does not allow the use of exclamation points within an SNMPv3 access password
metadata.excerpt: This is the result of a known issue. ... The Configuration utility does not allow you to create an SNMPv3 access password if it contains an exclamation point (!). ... If you attempt to create an SN...

content.sfdetails__c:
Known Issue

Note: This issue does not affect BIG-IP version 11.0.0 or later.

This is the result of a known issue. The Configuration utility does not allow you to create an SNMPv3 access password if it contains an exclamation point (!). If you attempt to create an SNMPv3 access password that contains an exclamation point, the following error message will be reported:

Bad Characters. Only the following special characters are allowed: period, asterisk, forward slash, dash, colon, underscore, question mark, equals, at sign, comma, ampersand and space (.*/-:_?=@,& )

F5 Product Development is tracking this issue as ID 347983.

Workaround

You must use tmsh through the command line to create a password that contains an exclamation point. To do so, perform one of the following two procedures:

Note: To view the current SNMP configuration before adding or modifying users, use the tmsh list sys snmp command.

Adding a new SNMP user for SNMPv3 access

  1. Log in to the BIG-IP command line.
  2. Enter the Traffic Management Shell by typing the following command:

    tmsh

  3. To enter the systems module, type the following command:

    sys

  4. To add a new user, use the following command syntax:

    modify snmp users add { <username> { <username> { access <ro | rw> auth-password <password> auth-protocol <md5 | sha> oid-subset <decimal numeric value> privacy-password <password> privacy-protocol <des> security-level auth-privacy username <username> } }

    For example:

    Note: The security level will default to no-auth-no-privacy if not specified. This level specifies that if the user cannot be authenticated, no access will be allowed. For more information about other options for the security level, refer to the Traffic Management Shell (tmsh) Reference Guide.

    (tmos.sys)# modify snmp users add { testuser { access ro auth-password !passwd! auth-protocol md5 oid-subset .1 privacy-password !passwd! privacy-protocol des security-level auth-privacy username testuser } }

  5. To exit the tmsh utility, type the following command:

    quit

Modifying an SNMP user for SNMPv3 access

  1. Log in to the BIG-IP command line.
  2. Enter the Traffic Management Shell by typing the following command:

    tmsh

  3. To enter the systems module, type the following command:

    sys

  4. To modify an existing user, use the following command syntax:

    modify snmp users modify { <username> { <username> { access <ro | rw> auth-password <password> auth-protocol <md5 | sha> oid-subset <decimal numeric value> privacy-password <password> privacy-protocol <des> security-level auth-privacy username <username> } }

    For example:

    Note: The security level will default to no-auth-no-privacy if not specified. This level specifies that if the user cannot be authenticated, no access will be allowed. For more information about other options for the security level, refer to the Traffic Management Shell (tmsh) Reference Guide.

    (tmos.sys)# modify snmp users add { testuser1 { access ro auth-password !mypasswd! auth-protocol md5 oid-subset .1 privacy-password !mypasswd! privacy-protocol des security-level auth-privacy username testuser1 } }

  5. To exit the tmsh utility, type the following command:

    quit
